Tests object coercion in JavaScript by verifying the result of adding an object to a string.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which are used to control how the object behaves in type conversion operations. The `valueOf` method returns the number `1`, and the `toString` method returns the string `"0"`. The code then uses an `assert` statement to verify that when the object is concatenated with the string `"1"`, the result is the string `"11"`. This demonstrates how JavaScript's type coercion rules apply when an object is involved in an operation with a string, prioritizing the `toString` method over `valueOf` in this context.
